heading: What are the properties of waves?
definition: Waves are disturbances that transfer energy from one point to another without transferring matter. All waves can be reflected, refracted, diffracted, and can interfere with each other.

concepts.title: Reflection
concepts.description: Waves bounce off a surface. Angle of incidence equals angle of reflection.

concepts.title: Refraction
concepts.description: Waves change speed and direction when moving between media of different densities.

concepts.title: Diffraction & Interference
concepts.description: Waves spread around obstacles and can combine constructively or destructively.

variableDefs:
v = wave speed (m/s)
f = frequency (Hz)
λ = wavelength (m)
T = period (s)
n = refractive index
